rad_findings_en:
FINDINGS:

Lungs and pleura: Pulmonary nodules in series 4 are as follows:
Right upper lobe:
- Faint ground-glass nodule measuring 6 mm on image 61.
Right middle lobe:
- Solid subpleural nodule measuring 3 mm on image 200.
Right lower lobe:
- Solid subpleural nodule measuring 3 mm on image 216.
Left upper lobe:
- Solid subpleural nodule measuring 3 mm on image 101.
- Solid subpleural nodule measuring 3 mm on image 145.
In both lungs, solid micronodules measure up to 2 mm.
In the right upper and middle lobes, there are areas of consolidation and ground-glass opacity, predominantly linear in configuration, series 4, images 113-193.
In the right upper and middle lobes (at the level of the above-described areas of increased lung opacity), lumens of dilated bronchi are present.
In both lungs, there are a few cysts, the largest in the right upper lobe measuring up to 1.9 × 1.3 cm, series 4, images 44-52.
In the right middle lobe, there is a calcified granuloma measuring up to 3 mm.
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly.
Moderate pericardial effusion measuring up to 1 cm.
No coronary calcification.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated, mild wall calcification.
Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.
Decreased height of the T7 and T8 vertebral bodies by 15% and 10%, respectively.

Upper abdomen: In the left kidney, there are two adjacent rounded lesions (or one "figure-8" lesion) with density up to 9 HU, measuring up to 2.6 × 2.8 cm and 2.8 × 2.5 cm.
rad_impression_en:
1. Solid micronodules in both lungs and solid nodules in the right middle and lower lobes and left upper lobe: per 2017 Fleischner Society guidelines, in high-risk patients, follow-up chest CT in 12 months; in low-risk patients, no follow-up is required.
Ground-glass nodule in the right upper lobe: per 2017 Fleischner Society guidelines, CT at 6–12 months to confirm persistence, then CT every 2 years until 5 years.
Calcified granuloma in the right middle lobe, consistent with prior infection.
A few pulmonary cysts.

2. Previously reported opacities in the right lung on the chest radiograph dated February 26, 2026 correspond on the current study to areas of consolidation and ground-glass opacity, more likely representing sequelae of prior multilobar pneumonia.
Bronchiectasis in the right upper and middle lobes.

3. Moderate pericardial effusion.

4. Mild atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

5. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.
Mild decrease in height of the T7 and T8 vertebral bodies; no further evaluation is required.

6. Hypodense left renal lesions: per Management of the Incidental Renal Mass on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee, 2018, no further evaluation is required.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Pulmonary nodules in series 4 are as follows:
Right upper lobe:
- Faint ground-glass nodule measuring 6 mm on image 61.
Right lower lobe:
- Solid subpleural nodule measuring 3 mm on image 216.
In both lungs, solid micronodules measure up to 2 mm.
In the upper and middle lobes of the right lung, linear atelectasis and ground-glass opacity are present (series 4, images 113–193).
A few cysts are present in both lungs, the largest in the right upper lobe measuring up to 1.9 × 1.3 cm (series 4, images 44–52).
Calcified granuloma in the right middle lobe measures up to 3 mm.
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. Moderate pericardial effusion measuring up to 7 mm. No coronary calcification.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated, with mild wall calcification. Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

Upper abdomen: In the mid poles of the left kidney, there are two cysts measuring 2.6 × 2.8 cm and 3.3 × 2.8 cm.
val_impression_en:
1. Solid nodules in both lungs and ground-glass nodule in the right upper lobe.  ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following: For patients with low risk of lung cancer, no need for further follow-up. For patients with high risk of lung cancer, generally no need for follow-up. An optional follow-up chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion. If unchanged, no further follow-up is necessary.

2. A few bilateral pulmonary cysts.

3. Previously reported opacities in the right lung on the chest radiograph dated February 26, 2026 correspond on the current study with areas of atelectasis and ground-glass opacity, most likely representing sequelae of prior multilobar pneumonia.

4. Moderate pericardial effusion.

5. Mild atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

6. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

7. Hypodense left renal lesions. ACR White Paper recommendations (Herts, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2018;15:264-273) suggest the following: Likely benign cyst. No need for further work-up.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura:
Pulmonary nodules on series 5 are as follows:
Right lower lobe:
- Solid nodule 4 mm, image 150 - unchanged.
Left lower lobe:
- Solid nodule up to 5 mm, image 216 - unchanged.
Left upper lobe:
- Solid nodule 4 mm, image 149 - unchanged.
In both lungs in the upper and lower lobes, multiple micronodules, including calcified, up to 2 mm - unchanged.
In the lungs along the interlobar pleura, polygonal/flattened nodules - on the right measuring 4 mm, image 129, 3 mm, image 128, 6 mm, image 148; on the left 5 mm, image 144, likely intrapulmonary lymph nodes - unchanged.
Mild apical fibrosis on the right and left.
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass and no lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated. Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.
A few calcifications of the aortic arch.

Skeletal structures and chest wall:
Combined treatment for right breast cancer, metallic inclusions in the breast and axillary region.
Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

Upper abdomen:
In the liver, lesions are present - hypodense, homogeneous, with density no more than 3 HU in segment 2 measuring 5.1 cm in diameter, in segment 4b measuring 9 mm in diameter - unchanged.
rad_impression_en:
1. A few solid nodules, unchanged compared with March 11, 2025. Fleischner Society recommendations may not be applicable considering the clinical history. Follow-up CT in 12 months.

2. Multiple small micronodules (including calcified) and solid nodules consistent with intrathoracic lymph nodes, unchanged compared with March 11, 2025.

3. Mild apical fibrosis on the right and left; no CT evidence of severe interstitial lung disease, unchanged compared with March 11, 2025.

4. A few liver lesions with benign features, likely cysts, unchanged compared with March 11, 2025; no follow-up is required per Management of Incidental Liver Lesions on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee, 2017.

5. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura:
Pulmonary nodules on series 5 are as follows:
Right lower lobe:
- Solid nodule 4 mm, image 151 - unchanged.
Right middle lobe:
- Solid nodule 4 mm, image 157 - unchanged.
Left lower lobe:
- Solid nodule up to 5 mm, image 216 - unchanged.
Left upper lobe:
- Solid nodule 4 mm, image 149 - unchanged.
In both lungs in the upper and lower lobes, multiple micronodules, including calcified, up to 2 mm - unchanged.
In the lungs along the interlobar pleura, polygonal/flattened nodules - on the right measuring 4 mm, image 129, 3 mm, image 128, 6 mm, image 148; on the left 5 mm, image 144, likely intrapulmonary lymph nodes - unchanged.
Multiple bilateral calcified granulomas up to 3 mm.
Linear atelectasis in the right middle lobe.
Mild apical fibrosis on the right and left.
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass and no lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated. Main pulmonary artery is not dilated. A few calcifications of the aortic arch.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Breast lumpectomy and radiation therapy, with metallic surgical clips present in the right breast and axillary region (unchanged). Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

Upper abdomen:
Liver hypodense lesions are present:
- In segment 2 measuring 5.1 cm in diameter - unchanged.
- In segment 4B measuring 9 mm in diameter - unchanged.
Moderate hepatic steatosis - unchanged.
val_impression_en:
1. A few solid nodules, unchanged compared with March 11, 2025. Fleischner Society recommendations are not applicable given the clinical history. Follow-up should be conducted according to oncologic surveillance protocols.

2. Multiple small micronodules (including calcified) and solid nodules consistent with intrathoracic lymph nodes, unchanged compared with March 11, 2025.

3. Mild apical fibrosis on the right and left; no CT evidence of severe interstitial lung disease, unchanged compared with March 11, 2025.

4. A few liver lesions with benign features, likely cysts, unchanged compared with March 11, 2025;  ACR White Paper recommendations (Gore, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2017;14:1429-1437) suggest the following: Benign lesion. No need for further follow-up. Moderate hepatic steatosis.

5. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Pleuroapical scarring bilaterally. In the lingular segments of both lungs, discoid atelectasis and traction bronchiectasis. Bronchial walls are irregularly thickened; bronchial contents in some segmental bronchi. Mild centrilobular emphysema in both lungs. Small nodules in both lungs with a "tree-in-bud" pattern. Micronodules in both lungs measuring up to 3 mm in diameter. Small ground-glass opacities in the left lower lobe.  
Pulmonary nodules in series 5 are as follows:  
Right upper lobe:  
- Part-solid nodule measuring 5 mm on image 86.  
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as mild. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for a comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Note: Recommendation applies unless the patient has known CAD, prior CABG/stent, trauma indications or intraoperative CTs. Aortic valve calcification. Pacemaker implantation.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated. Calcified atherosclerotic plaques in the wall of the thoracic aorta. C-shaped tortuosity of the thoracic aorta. Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Hardware in the C6 vertebral body.

Upper abdomen: No abnormality.
rad_impression_en:
1. Numerous bilateral pulmonary nodules, with the largest in the left lower lobe, part-solid, measuring up to 5 mm, with progression compared with April 10, 2025 and October 07, 2025. Per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, chest CT in 12 months.

2. CT findings of left lower lobe bronchopneumonia.

3. CT findings of bronchiolitis.

4. Bronchiectasis in both lungs. Discoid atelectasis in both lungs.

5. Mild pulmonary emphysema. Pulmonary emphysema on CT is an independent risk factor for lung cancer. Consider enrollment in a low-dose CT (LDCT) lung cancer screening program if the patient meets eligibility criteria; today's CT may serve as a baseline for future screening. Note: Recommendation applies unless the patient has a history of lung cancer, known pulmonary nodules or lung mass, receives routine chest CTs/screening, or is receiving palliative care.

6. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as mild. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for a comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Note: Recommendation applies unless the patient has known CAD, prior CABG/stent, trauma indications or intraoperative CTs. Aortic valve calcification. Pacemaker implantation.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Biapical pleural-parenchymal scarring, small areas of paraseptal emphysema. There is persistent consolidation involving the middle lobe and lingula. Multiple tree-in-bud nodules are scattered throughout both lungs, compared to Chest CT on October 7, new centrilobular areas of ground-glass opacity, predominantly in the lower left lobe, for example, on images 138, 175. Segmental bronchi with thickened walls. Subsegmental bronchi with thickened walls, large volumes of mucus, and the formation of bronchiectasis and bronchocele. 
Pulmonary nodules in series 5 are as follows:  
Right upper lobe:  
- New part-solid nodule measures 5 mm on image 86, likely centrilobular, inflammatory. 
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. Enlargement of the left ventricle. No significant pericardial effusion. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left main, left anterior descending, left circumflex arteries, qualitatively assessed as mild. Aortic valve calcifications. Pacemaker. 

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated. Calcified atherosclerotic plaques in the wall of the thoracic aorta. C-shaped tortuosity of the thoracic aorta. Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Healing fracture of the 6th rib on the right. Haller index 3.4 (pectus excavatum). 

Upper abdomen: No abnormality.
val_impression_en:
1. Bilateral consolidation, multiple tree-in-bud nodules, bronchial wall thickening with mucous plugging and bronchiectasis predominantly involving the right middle lobe and lingula consistent with atypical mycobacterial infection (MAC/MAI). Interval worsening compared to CT on October 7, 2025, due to the appearance of new centrilobular ground-glass nodules. 
New part-solid nodule measures 5 mm in the right upper lobe, likely centrilobular, inflammatory. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following: No need for routine follow-up. However, if the nodule has suspicious morphology or if the patient belongs to a higher-risk population group, consider follow-up chest CT at 2 and 4 years.

2. Mild paraseptal pulmonary emphysema. Pulmonary emphysema on CT is an independent risk factor for lung cancer. Consider enrollment in a low-dose CT (LDCT) lung cancer screening program if the patient meets eligibility criteria; today's CT may serve as a baseline for future screening. Note: Recommendation applies unless the patient has a history of lung cancer, known pulmonary nodules or lung mass, receives routine chest CTs/screening, or is receiving palliative care.

3. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as mild. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for a comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Note: Recommendation applies unless the patient has known CAD, prior CABG/stent, trauma indications or intraoperative CTs. Aortic valve calcification. Enlargement of the left ventricle. 


DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura:
Pulmonary nodules on series 4 are as follows:
Right upper lobe:
- Subsolid perifissural nodule measuring 4 mm on image 30.
- Solid perifissural nodule measuring 5 mm on image 33.
- Solid nodule measuring 4 mm on image 45.
Left upper lobe:
- Solid micronodules measuring up to 2-3 mm.
The nodule in the left lower lobe is not identified on the current examination.
Calcification in the right upper lobe measuring 6 mm on image 81.
Areas of centrilobular emphysema in the upper lobe parenchyma.
Reticular changes in the peripheral lower lobes with a few areas of honeycombing and a few bronchiolectases.
Parenchymal bands in segment 5 of the left lung, unchanged.
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. No coronary artery calcification.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated. Main pulmonary artery is not dilated. Moderate aortic calcification. Moderate aortic valve calcification.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Severe degenerative changes of the spine - spondylosis, osteochondrosis; moderate spondyloarthrosis.

Upper abdomen: A few calcifications in the splenic parenchyma.
rad_impression_en:
1. Solid pulmonary nodules measuring less than 6 mm are stable compared with the prior study. According to Fleischner Society 2017 recommendations, additional CT follow-up is not recommended.

2. Findings of fibrosing interstitial lung disease, CT pattern of usual interstitial pneumonia.

3. Mild centrilobular emphysema.

4. Aortic valve calcification.

5. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

6. A few calcifications in the splenic parenchyma - postinflammatory granulomas.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura:
Pulmonary nodules on series 4 are as follows:
Right upper lobe:
- Solid perifissural nodule measuring 4 mm on image 30 (unchanged).
- Solid perifissural nodule measuring 5 mm on image 33 (unchanged).
- Solid nodule measuring 4 mm on image 45 (unchanged).
Left upper lobe: 
- Ground glass nodule measuring 6 mm on image 134 (unchanged).
Multiple bilateral solid micronodules measuring up to 3 mm (unchanged).
The nodule in the left lower lobe is not identified on the current examination.
ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following: No need for further follow-up for patients with high or low risk of lung cancer.
Calcified granuloma in the right upper lobe measuring 6 mm. Mild bilateral centrilobular emphysema. Linear atelectasis, predominantly in the basal segments of the lungs. Reticular changes in the peripheral lower lobes with scattered areas of honeycombing and a few bronchiolectases. There is progression with increased reticulation and the development of bronchiolectases. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy. Few calcified lymph nodes are noted in the paratracheal group and at the right lung hilum, likely representing prior granulomatous disease.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. No coronary artery calcification. Moderate aortic valve calcification. 

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated. Main pulmonary artery is not dilated. Moderate aortic calcification. 

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Decreased bone density in the vertebrae, most pronounced in the T12 vertebral body, measuring up to +102 HU.

Upper abdomen: A few calcifications in the splenic parenchyma, likely representing prior granulomatous disease. Gallbladder stone measuring 4 × 5 mm (not fully included in the scan range).
val_impression_en:
1. Typical UIP CT pattern. Progression of interstitial lung disease is noted, with increased reticulation and newly developed bronchiolectases.

2. Pulmonary nodules. No need for further follow-up.

3. Mild centrilobular emphysema. Pulmonary emphysema on CT is an independent risk factor for lung cancer. Consider enrollment in a low-dose CT (LDCT) lung cancer screening program if the patient meets eligibility criteria; today's CT may serve as a baseline for future screening. Note: Recommendation applies unless the patient has a history of lung cancer, known pulmonary nodules or lung mass, receives routine chest CTs/screening, or is receiving palliative care.

4. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta. Aortic valve calcification.

5. Gallbladder stone.

6. CT signs of bone demineralization.

7. Few calcified lymph nodes, likely representing prior granulomatous disease.

8. Few calcifications in the splenic parenchyma, likely representing prior granulomatous disease.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Lung nodules in series 5 are as follows:
Right upper lobe:
- Complex solid nodule with irregular margins measuring 2.4 × 1.7 cm on image 50, with fluid density in the central portions and gas inclusion.
- Solid nodule with irregular margins measuring 1.8 × 1.3 cm on image 49.
- Micronodule on image 47.
Linear pleural tags are present at the periphery of the right upper lobe lesions.
Areas of centrilobular and paraseptal emphysema, predominantly in the upper lobes.
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. Severe coronary calcification is present in the left and right main coronary arteries, anterior descending and circumflex branches of the left coronary artery, and diagonal branch of the anterior descending artery.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated. Main pulmonary artery is dilated to 3.3 cm. Mild calcification of the aortic wall. Mild calcification of the aortic valve.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Severe degenerative changes of the spine: osteochondrosis, spondylosis. Decreased density of vertebral bodies to 100-120 HU.

Upper abdomen: Decreased density of the liver parenchyma to 40 HU.
rad_impression_en:
1. Solid and complex solid nodules in the right upper lobe, possibly neoplastic. Inflammatory process cannot be excluded. PET/CT may be performed for further characterization.

2. Mild mixed pulmonary emphysema.

3. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as severe. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for a comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

4. Aortic valve calcification.

5. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

6. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.3 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

7. Hepatic steatosis.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Moderate centrilobular and mild paraseptal emphysema, predominantly in the upper lobes. In the right lower lobe there is a paravertebral area of osteophyte-induced fibrosis. Lung nodules in the right upper lobe in series 5 are as follows:
- Cavitary nodule with irregular margins measures 2.5 × 1.6 cm (image 50), with fluid density in the central part, peripheral bronchiectasis and cords to the pleura.
- Solid nodule with irregular margins measures 1.8 × 1.3 cm (image 49), adjacent to the pleura.
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. Severe coronary calcification is present in the left and right main coronary arteries, anterior descending and circumflex branches of the left coronary artery, and diagonal branch of the anterior descending artery. Aortic valve calcifications.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated. Main pulmonary artery is dilated to 3.3 cm. Mild calcification of the aortic wall.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the spine. Decreased density of vertebral bodies to 100 HU (CT signs of demineralization).

Upper abdomen: Decreased density of the liver parenchyma to 40 HU (hepatic steatosis).
val_impression_en:
1. In the right upper lobe, cavitary and solid nodules are present.
Most likely represents an infectious or inflammatory process, although a neoplasm is also possible.

2. Moderate centrilobular and mild paraseptal emphysema. Pulmonary emphysema on CT is an independent risk factor for lung cancer. Consider enrollment in a low-dose CT (LDCT) lung cancer screening program if the patient meets eligibility criteria; today's CT may serve as a baseline for future screening. Note: Recommendation applies unless the patient has a history of lung cancer, known pulmonary nodules or lung mass, receives routine chest CTs/screening, or is receiving palliative care.

3. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as severe. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for a comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Aortic valve calcification. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

4. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.3 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
